MODULAR ECOSYSTEMS // ZPWR-PATCH-CORE PORT REPORT

Docs / Catalog

// VCV Rack + Cherry Audio Voltage Modular + Eurorack hardware, each module mapped to the zpc block for its FUNCTION - a function-coverage audit, validated against the real block list.

Catalog

⚠ Read this first - what this report is, and isn't

This is a function-presence map, not a 1:1 port and not a feature-parity claim. All mappings collapse onto only 110 distinct zpc blocks (of zpc’s 4238); the top 10 absorb 55%. There are two honesty caveats: (1) 6110 MAPPED = the module’s function is a specific category zpc has real depth in (oscillator, filter, reverb, sequencer...). (2) 691 CATEGORY-ONLY = the module only carried a vague tag (Effect / Utility / Processor / Source / CV) so all it proves is ‘zpc has something in that bucket’ — NOT that zpc does this module’s job. There is no ‘0 gaps’ claim: a per-module gap can’t be measured at this granularity (I’m not comparing 7000 modules feature-by-feature), and the gap metric would be circular since zpc has one block per category by construction. What IS true: every module resolves to a real zpc block (validated vs BLOCKS.md, 0 invalid) or an architectural N/A. Real feature gaps, if any, hide inside the CATEGORY-ONLY rows — sort by status to inspect them.

Where it all lands — the many-to-one collapse

683→ StepSeq
667→ VCO
378→ Ladder
347→ Mixer
258→ Analyzer
240→ Logic
231→ ADSR
193→ Delay
188→ Switch
162→ Kick
152→ Scaler
147→ Arp
6110 modules → 110 blocks. The other ~1309 zpc blocks aren’t even referenced — one block per function is enough to ‘cover’ a whole category.
7374modules audited
110distinct zpc blocks
6110specific-function MAPPED
691CATEGORY-ONLY (unverified)
573architectural N/A
0invalid / faked

By ecosystem

VCV 4605 modules
3972 mapped · 320 category-only · 313 N/A · by function tag (manifest)
Voltage Modular 2499 modules
1891 mapped · 360 category-only · 248 N/A · by function category (store)
Eurorack hardware 270 modules
247 mapped · 11 category-only · 12 N/A · by name (ModularGrid clone-index; the famous hardware, not the full ~15k catalog)
